footnotes.text: Has the greater sin: in condemning Christ, Pilate was guilty of a sin against justice. Caiphas, however, sinned against both religion and justice. Pilate's offense was modified by his great fear and weakness.

Judgment-seat: a rostrum or curule chair from which such sentences were officially pronounced. Lithostrotos: a stone pavement which, from Gabbatha (an elevated or cleared space), we may judge was a courtyard outside the praetorium.
That their legs might be broken: this practice was used when there was any reason for hastening the death of the crucified.
